titleMn: Өдөр 22: Өчигдөр ба өнгөрсөн долоо хоног
objectiveEn: About one hour. Learn 20 past time words, Past Simple of 'to be' (was/were), reading, speaking, listening, and homework.
objectiveMn: Ойролцоогоор 1 цаг. 20 өнгөрсөн цагийн үг, was/were дүрэм, унших, ярих, сонсох, гэрийн даалгавар.
contentEn: Learn how to talk about yesterday and last week using was and were, with reading and listening practice about a sick day.
contentMn: Was, were ашиглан өчигдөр болон өнгөрсөн долоо хоногийн тухай ярьж сурна; өвчтэй өдрийн тухай унших, сонсох дасгал.

vocabulary.example: Three days ago I was sick.
I was busy all day yesterday.
Before lunch I was at school.
I was at home all day.

quiz.question: I _____ tired yesterday.
quiz.explanation: Use WAS with I in the past.

quiz.question: _____ she at school yesterday?
quiz.explanation: Past question with she uses WAS.

quiz.question: We _____ ready — we were early!
quiz.explanation: Negative for 'we' in past is WEREN'T.

quiz.question: Where was Bat yesterday?
quiz.explanation: Bat was at home because he was sick.

quiz.question: Why was Bat absent?
quiz.explanation: Bat was sick with a bad cold.

quiz.question: What is 'was not' in contracted form?
What was yesterday's lesson about?
quiz.explanation: The lesson was about Past Simple.

quiz.question: He _____ sick last week.
quiz.explanation: Use WAS with he in past.

page1.grammarTopic: Past Simple — was / were (To Be in the past)
page1.grammarExplanation: Past Simple of 'To Be' has two forms: WAS (for I, he, she, it — singular) and WERE (for you, we, they — plural). WAS and WERE are the past tense of IS/AM and ARE. Use them to describe states, feelings, locations, and conditions in the past. Negative: was not = wasn't / were not = weren't. Question: move WAS/WERE to the front of the sentence. 'To Be' үйл үгийн өнгөрсөн цаг: WAS (I, he, she, it — нэгдмэл) ба WERE (you, we, they — олон тоо). WAS/WERE нь IS/AM ба ARE-ийн өнгөрсөн цаг. Өнгөрсөн цагийн байдал, нөхцөл, байршлыг тодорхойлоход хэрэглэнэ. Үгүйсгэл: wasn't / weren't. Асуулт: WAS/WERE-г эхэнд тавь.
commonMistakes: I were sick → I WAS sick
They was late → They WERE late
Was they there? → WERE they there?
She were happy → She WAS happy

page2.readingPassage: Last weekend was very special. On Saturday, Bat and his family were at the park. The weather was sunny and warm. Bat's mother was very happy. His father was relaxed. On Sunday, Bat was at his grandmother's house. His cousins were there too. They were all together and very happy. Bat thinks: 'Last weekend was the best!'
speakingQuestions: Where were you yesterday? / Та өчигдөр хаана байсан бэ?
How was the weather last week?

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: Where were you yesterday? I didn't see you.

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: I was at home. I was sick.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: Oh no! Were you very sick?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: Yes, I was. I had a headache and a fever.

rolePlay.speaker: A
rolePlay.text: Was anyone at home with you?

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: Yes, my mother was with me all day.

rolePlay.speaker: B
rolePlay.text: It was great! The grammar was easy.

page2.speakingTip: Don't worry about mistakes — just keep talking! Every mistake helps you learn faster. Confidence is more important than perfection at this stage.

page3.listeningScript: Teacher: Good morning! Where were you yesterday, Bat? You weren't in class. Bat: I was at home, teacher. I was sick — I had a bad cold. Teacher: I'm sorry to hear that. Were you sick all day? Bat: Yes. I wasn't well at all. My mother was with me. Teacher: Sarnai, were you at school yesterday? Sarnai: Yes I was. The lesson was very interesting! It was about Past Simple. Teacher: That's right! And today Bat is back. Were you well this morning, Bat? Bat: Yes! I was fine. I wasn't late either. I'm happy to be back!

listeningQuestions.question: Was Bat alone at home?
options: no, his mother was with him
listeningQuestions.answer: no, his mother was with him

listeningQuestions.question: How does Bat feel about coming back?
What is 'was not' in contracted form?

grammarPractice: 5. She _____ at home — she was at school.
homework: Writing: Write 5 sentences about where you were last weekend using was/were.
Vocabulary: Write 5 sentences using 'last week', 'yesterday', 'ago' and was/were.
Speaking: Ask a friend: 'Where were you yesterday?' — use was/were in your answer.
Review: I/he/she/it = WAS · you/we/they = WERE · Negative: wasn't/weren't
completionSummary: Mastered was / were (past of To Be)
